mappings.rationaleAI: Introduces general conceptions of technology and engineering, relevant to A (definitions of technology) and the broad sweep of technological history.
Examines the transformation of 1750-1900 (C.3) including Watt's steam engine, Bessemer's steel process, textile mechanization, and the rise of industrial chemistry and communications.
Addresses B (technology and contemporary life) including technology's role in economic development and Schumpeter's concept of creative destruction.
Directly covers B.2 (technology and war) from ancient weapons through nuclear arms.
Addresses B.3 (technology and education) including the role of technology in transforming pedagogical methods and educational access.
Links technological change to wealth creation (B.1) and traces the sociocultural consequences of technological shifts across global history (C).
Directly relevant to B.7 (effects of technology on the environment) including fossil fuel combustion, industrial pollution, and the climate crisis.
Addresses B.7 (effects of technology on the environment) through the lens of humanity's accumulated technological impact on Earth systems.
Traces the beginnings of technology to c. 3000 BC (C.1.a), including Oldowan stone tools, fire use, and the earliest agricultural settlements.
Illuminates the technological achievements of Greece 500 BC-AD 500 (C.1.c), including mastery of iron smelting, the Antikythera mechanism, and monumental architecture.
Surveys medieval advances AD 500-1500 (C.2.a) including the heavy plough, wind and water mills, Gothic cathedrals, and advances in navigation and shipbuilding.
Explores the emergence of Western technology 1500-1750 (C.2.b), including the invention of the telescope, microscope, and other scientific instruments.
Traces computing from Babbage's Analytical Engine to the electronic digital computer, relevant to Space Age technology and automation (C.4.b).
Focuses on nuclear fission power development in the 20th century, a defining technology of the Space Age (C.4.b).
Addresses advances in power technology (C.3.a) and modern energy developments (C.4.b) including solar, wind, and hydroelectric power.
Spans advances in power technology (C.3.a) through 20th-century energy infrastructure (C.4), including fossil fuels, nuclear energy, and electrical grids.
Explores automation, autonomous machines, and space exploration robotics as defining Space Age technologies (C.4.b).
Traces AI from Turing's foundational ideas through machine learning, relevant to the electronic digital computer and automation (C.4.b).
Addresses B.7 (effects of technology on the environment) and B.6 (technology and underdeveloped regions).
Spans the urban revolution's waterworks and irrigation systems (C.1.b) through the architectural and constructional works of Greece and Rome (C.1.c).
